judgment.summary: Sandisk Corporation exhibits exceptional growth and profitability, supported by a strong balance sheet and positive analyst sentiment, though valuation metrics warrant attention.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter EPS of $23.41 beat the estimate of $14.66 by 59.7%. Next earnings date is 2026-04-30.
positives: Revenue growth of 251.0% indicates massive top-line expansion.
Profit margin of 34.2% and operating margin of 70.0% demonstrate exceptional profitability.
Return on equity of 39.3% and return on assets of 22.8% highlight efficient use of capital.
Current ratio of 4.78 and quick ratio of 3.43 suggest a very healthy short-term liquidity position.
Forward P/E of 7.94 is significantly lower than trailing P/E of 47.48, implying strong expected future earnings growth.
Analyst consensus is a buy with a mean target price of $1460.41, suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 1.50 indicates a notable level of financial leverage.
Price/Sales (TTM) of 15.64 is high, suggesting the market values each dollar of sales significantly.
Price/Book of 20.18 is also high, indicating the stock trades at a substantial premium to its book value.

news.paragraph: SanDisk is currently trading amidst a strong rally in chipmaker stocks, with headlines highlighting a significant price target increase from Citi and the CEO's view that the memory market will remain undersupplied, partly due to AI demand. The potential for a strike at Samsung is also contributing to upward pressure on memory chip prices, benefiting companies like SanDisk. While the company is mentioned in discussions about potential stock splits and as a comparison for AI infrastructure investments, there are no immediate earnings or specific product catalysts mentioned in these recent headlines.

headlines.title: Sandisk Stock Gets More Than $2,000 Price Target Boost From Citi
Micron Gains as Samsung Strike Nears
Sandisk CEO Says Memory Market Will Stay ‘Undersupplied.’ AI Is Changing the Business.
Nvidia, Micron and AMD Leads Chip Rally Samsung Strike Threat Shakes Chip Supply
Checking in On Red Hot AI Infrastructure Stocks
Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller Dumped Every Share of Alphabet. He Bought AI Memory Stocks Up 581% to 3,467% Instead
Stocks Climb on Lower Bond Yields and Chipmaker Strength
Will Goldman Sachs, Markel, or SanDisk Be the Next Big Stock Split?
Micron, SanDisk gain as Samsung workers on brink of strike
TTM Technologies vs. SanDisk: Which AI Infra Stock is a Better Buy?
